Subset(10) call HTTP200 routed; full-112 HTTP400 INVALID_ARGUMENT. Free-tier quota exhausted mid-probe (429) so remaining offenders characterized STATICALLY.

providers.prompt: solaris_build_graph forcing prompt (template=lighting_rig enum + 2 nodes each with nested parms{intensity 2.5 / exposure 1.0} + 1 connection object)
providers.h3_notes: AUTH-BLOCKED pre-tool-eval; no fidelity measurement possible with this credential

LOSSY (dropped optional/nested): name correct + enum 'lighting_rig' kept + connections nested object kept, BUT both nodes[].parms came back EMPTY {} -- the intensity:2.5 and exposure:1.0 values were DROPPED. Root cause: parms is a free-form type:object with no inner schema; translator sanitizes it to a nested OBJECT with empty properties{} and Gemini will not populate (and at full-112 will not even accept) a property-less nested OBJECT. (Auto-scorer flagged faithful=true checking only that parms was a dict; manual review = NOT faithful, values lost.) Gemini carries NO functionCall id (tool_id_present=false).

FAITHFUL: name correct, tool_id present, enum kept as enum, nested parms kept nested WITH values, connections nested, intensity stayed 2.5 (float). Only nit: exposure 1.0 -> 1 (JSON int/float collapse, semantically identical, not coercion-to-string). slow: 93.4s for nano local.

FAITHFUL: identical structural fidelity to local nano -- name correct, tool_id present, enum kept, nested parms with values, connections nested, intensity 2.5 float. exposure 1.0->1 int/float collapse only. 46.9s (550b cloud).

full_112_results.raw_error: AuthenticationError 401 invalid x-api-key (req_011Cc3nMiCkepapt6YDDEfDG) -- fails at credential layer before payload is evaluated; says NOTHING about whether Anthropic would accept the 112-tool/~63KB payload. Anthropic's documented tool limit is far above 112 tools, so size is not expected to be a problem; UNVERIFIED here.

First attempt (enum-on-string-only NOT yet applied): HTTP400 with explicit field path -> tools[0].function_declarations[88].parameters.properties[1].value.enum[0] (TYPE_STRING),0 / enum[1],1. function_declarations[88] = synapse_safe_render, property soho_foreground is an INTEGER enum [0,1]; Gemini OpenAPI subset allows enum only on STRING. After fixing translator to drop enum on non-STRING types: still HTTP400 but now generic 'Request contains an invalid argument' (INVALID_ARGUMENT, no field). Static scan of the 112 sanitized decls found the next-layer cause: 12 tools carry a property-less nested OBJECT (free-form type:object slot, no inner schema) -- e.g. synapse_solaris_build_graph.nodes[].parms & .template_params, houdini_render_settings.settings, synapse_memory_write.content, tops_multi_shot.shots[].overrides -- which Gemini rejects as nested OBJECT without properties; AND 3 tools (houdini_set_parm.value, houdini_set_usd_attribute.value, tops_query_items.filter_value) have an UNTYPED 'any' slot the translator must DROP (Gemini requires a type on every property). NET: full-112 fails for Gemini due to translation incompatibility, NOT payload size (63KB well within limits). Quota note: per-tool isolation loop hit free-tier 429 after ~8 calls, so the 104/112 'rejections' in that loop are 429 quota NOT schema -- disregard.

full_112_results.note: Full 112-tool / OpenAI-tools payload accepted cleanly; on the trivial ping prompt it correctly selected synapse_ping. No size/schema rejection.
Full 112-tool payload accepted; correctly selected synapse_ping on the ping prompt.

hypothesis_verdict:
PER PROVIDER (verdict from THIS measured data):

ANTHROPIC -> INCONCLUSIVE (credential-blocked), defaults to H1 by design. Both subset and full-112 calls returned 401 invalid x-api-key on a credential that is the Claude Code CLI token, not a raw API key (confirmed: bare no-tools call also 401). No tool-fidelity could be measured. The registry wire shape ({name,description,input_schema}, built in tool_bridge.py:56-60 by renaming inputSchema->input_schema and dropping annotations) is NATIVE to Anthropic, and Anthropic's documented tool ceiling is well above 112 tools / 63KB, so H1 (full schema clean) is the EXPECTED outcome -- but UNVERIFIED here. Not H2, not H3 on available evidence.

GEMINI -> H3 CONFIRMED (translation lossy), with a full-payload rejection that is ALSO translation-rooted, not size-rooted. Evidence: (1) Subset call routed the correct tool NAME but DROPPED the nested parms object values (returned parms:{} instead of {intensity:2.5}/{exposure:1.0}) -- strict round-trip validation FAILS. (2) Full-112 rejected HTTP400 first on an integer-enum (synapse_safe_render.soho_foreground enum[0,1] -- Gemini allows enum only on STRING), then after that fix on property-less nested OBJECTs (12 tools) and dropped untyped 'any' slots (3 tools incl. houdini_set_parm.value). The 63KB/112-tool payload is within Gemini limits, so NOT H2 (size) -- it is H3 (lossy Anthropic->Gemini translation) escalated to a hard rejection at full scale.

OLLAMA LOCAL (nemotron-3-nano) -> H1. Subset call: correct name, tool_id present, enum preserved as enum, nested parms preserved WITH values, connections nested, numerics intact (only 1.0->1 int/float collapse). Full-112 accepted, correct trivial selection. No degradation past N. Strict round-trip PASSES.

OLLAMA CLOUD (nemotron-3-ultra:cloud) -> H1. Identical structural fidelity to local; full-112 accepted; correct trivial selection. H1.

CROSS-CUTTING: H2 (degrades past N tools -> router) is REFUTED for the two providers fully exercised (both Ollama accepted all 112). H2 UNTESTED for Anthropic (auth) and does not apply to Gemini (Gemini's full-112 failure is H3-translation, not N-count degradation -- the SAME constructs fail at N=1).

per_provider_native_vs_compat.anthropic: NATIVE (by construction, unverified live). The registry already serializes to Anthropic's exact {name,description,input_schema} shape via tool_bridge.py:56-60 -- this IS the native path, needs no translation. No OpenAI-compat layer applies. Live fidelity unverified only because this environment lacks a valid raw API key (env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is the CLI credential, 401). Recommendation: native; a real API key is the only blocker to confirming H1.
per_provider_native_vs_compat.gemini: NEITHER pure-native NOR clean-OpenAI-compat suffices -- requires a DEDICATED, LOSS-AWARE TRANSLATION + REPAIR layer, OR avoid Gemini for tool-calling against this registry. Measured: naive input_schema->functionDeclaration translation is LOSSY (drops untyped 'value' slots, empties free-form object slots like parms/content/settings) and the full registry is OUTRIGHT REJECTED (integer enums, property-less nested objects). To use Gemini you must (a) stringify-or-drop non-STRING enums, (b) synthesize a minimal {type:STRING} or a real inner schema for every free-form type:object slot so it is never a property-less OBJECT, (c) give untyped 'any' slots a concrete type (e.g. STRING) instead of dropping them. Until then Gemini cannot losslessly drive houdini_set_parm/houdini_set_usd_attribute/tops_query_items (value dropped) nor any tool with an opaque object slot (value silently empty). Gemini's native functionDeclaration format is the right target, but the registry JSON-Schema must be REPAIRED for the OpenAPI subset, not just renamed.
per_provider_native_vs_compat.ollama_local: OpenAI-tools COMPAT, verbatim pass-through. The registry JSON-Schema inputSchema embeds directly as function.parameters with ZERO repair and was fully faithful (nested objects, enums, numerics, all 112 accepted). No native Ollama format needed; the OpenAI /api/chat tools shape is sufficient and lossless. Use OpenAI-compat.
per_provider_native_vs_compat.ollama_cloud: OpenAI-tools COMPAT, verbatim pass-through (server proxies to ollama.com). Same as local: zero-repair JSON-Schema pass-through, fully faithful, all 112 accepted. Use OpenAI-compat.

notes:
METHOD INTEGRITY: Used the REAL 112-tool registry under PY631 (synapse.mcp._tool_registry.TOOL_JSON, 112 entries confirmed), not synthesized schemas. Subset = the 10 distinct baton tool names; forcing target = synapse_solaris_build_graph (the deep array+nested-object+enum case). Probe script written OUTSIDE the repo at C:\\Users\\User\\probe_tmp\\synapse_fidelity_probe.py (the harness-specified %TEMP% under AppData is write-blocked by the file tool, so a sibling non-repo dir was used; full payload 63238 bytes ~= baton's ~65.7KB). NO source files edited.

KEY CAVEAT ON GEMINI auto-scorer: score_args() marked Gemini faithful=true because it only checked parms was a dict; manual inspection shows parms came back EMPTY ({}), so nested values were DROPPED. Corrected verdict = faithful=FALSE for Gemini. The Ollama legs were genuinely faithful (values present). Surfacing this rather than trusting the auto-flag.

ANTHROPIC BLOCKER: the only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY available (108-char sk-ant-api03-...) returns 401 invalid x-api-key on every call including a bare no-tools call -- it is the Claude Code CLI credential, not a raw API key. daemon resolves the same env var (auth.py:101) so there is no alternate. To verify Anthropic H1 live, a real Anthropic API key must be exported.

ROUTER_REQUIRED=true RATIONALE: a router/translation-repair layer IS required, but provider-specifically. NOT needed for Anthropic (native shape) or Ollama local/cloud (OpenAI-compat JSON-Schema pass-through was lossless and accepted all 112). IS required for Gemini, which (a) drops untyped 'value' slots and empties free-form object slots in translation (lossy at N=1) and (b) outright rejects the full 112-tool payload (integer enums + property-less nested objects). So router_required=true is driven entirely by the Gemini leg. If Gemini is dropped from the provider set, router_required would be FALSE on the measured evidence.

LATENCY (ms, subset arg-fidelity call): anthropic 217 (401 fast-fail), gemini 3265, ollama_local 93404 (nano local, slow), ollama_cloud 46860 (550b). Full-112 trivial call: anthropic 184 (401), gemini 531 (400), ollama_local 11176, ollama_cloud 5685.

MODEL STRINGS RESOLVED LIVE: gemini-flash-latest (also gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-3.5-flash); nemotron-3-nano:latest local + nemotron-3-ultra:cloud (550b, ctx 262144), both tools-capable per /api/tags. DEFAULT_MODEL seed divergence (sonnet-4-5 vs -4-6) not load-bearing here, not chased.

ARTIFACTS: probe script preserved at C:\\Users\\User\\probe_tmp\\synapse_fidelity_probe.py (outside repo). Transient Gemini retest/bisect/static-scan scripts removed after use.
